children.title: Retroactive Anger In Breakups
children.description: An exploration of the emotional journey that can be prompted by a tough breakup.

children.title: What is a relationship ontologically?
children.description: An attempt at organizing some of my thoughts of what kind of ontological entity a relationship may be, and why it has important consequences for the properties of relationships.

children.title: What does it mean to be hard to love?
children.description: Love and care goes both ways: an exploration into "being hard to love".

children.title: A-Spec Identity & Sexual Attraction Within Relationships
children.description: How can we understand sexual attraction and its relation to A-Spec identity within the context of relationships?

children.title: Vulnerability, Intimacy, and Love
children.description: My take on how vulnerability creates intimacy, which is the key part of love.

children.title: Relationships, Partnership, And Companionship
children.description: What is the difference between partnership and companionship, if anything? Does it matter?

children.title: What is a situationship?
children.description: Can we create a definition of situationship that captures ordinary linguistic usage?

children.title: Compatibility, Meeting Needs, and ENM
children.description: Within the paradigm of non-monogamy, how can we know if we are compatible with someone/when to start a relationship with them?

children.title: Long-Term vs Daily Life Alignment
children.description: A look into two different, but related modes of alignment and its relation to general compatibility.

children.title: My Take on a Relationship Anarchy Worksheet
children.description: A look at what a RA worksheet designed by me might look like.

children.title: The RA Smörgåsbord and the Worksheet-ification of Relationship Anarchy
children.description: A list of my critiques on RA worksheets and how they may lead to shallower engagement with RA principles.

children.title: Thoughts on Polynormativity
children.description: Drawing together my thoughts on polynormativity through a few different articles; some that I've written, some that I have not.

children.title: The Emotionality Gap in Relationships
children.description: An underdiscussed potential gap that can be felt in relationships, especially heterosexual ones.

children.title: How To Build (Important) Relationship Skills
children.description: A list of foundational beliefs and skills that will make any/all of your relationships better and deeper.

children.title: What does growing together in a relationship mean?
children.description: Comparing three possible senses of the phrase where none of them are "incorrect", but will depend on a per-relationship basis.

children.title: Commitment Without Escalation
children.description: Exploring a model separate from partnership to ascribing importance to certain relationships in our lives.

children.title: One Love
children.description: My argument for there not being any different types of love.

children.title: Are shared goals required for a partnership?
children.description: My take on why shared goals aren't required for a partnership, but that shared values and intentions are more important.

children.title: Is non-monogamous love shallower?
children.description: I think that this is a question that monogamous and non-monogamous people have all thought about at least once, so I wanted to dedicate some time thinking about it to settle the question.

children.title: On Training Men To Be Boyfriends
children.description: An attempt to explain and ameloriate the experience of "training" your boyfriend.

children.title: Asexuality and Sexual Desire/Pleasure
children.description: In this article, I argue for a more pleasure-centric understanding of asexuality as a way to make introspection and identification easier.

children.title: Why Do People Stay In Bad Relationships?
children.description: Exploring the microeconomics/behavioral economics of staying in a bad relationship.

children.title: Re-imagining vs De-centering Romance
children.description: Can you re-imagine romance without de-centering it or vice versa?

children.title: Polyamory Acts on More Than Just Romance
children.description: I think that the greatest benefit of non-monogamy is not just increased romance in your life, but increased potential for closeness with all types of relationships.

children.title: Attached by Amir Levine and Rachel S. F. Heller
children.description: Based on the science of attachment, the book looks at the three basic types of attachment: avoidant, anxious, and secure.

children.title: Romance Is Prescriptive
children.description: Romance has prescriptive power which can limit your relationships.

children.title: Can we reimagine romance?
children.description: The concept of romance doesn't always serve everyone. Can we rehabilitate this concept or are we better off abandoning it entirely?

children.title: Everything is not romantic
children.description: My proposal for getting away from using the word "romance" to avoid conceptual baggage and adding to the weight of the term.

children.title: Resisting the Automatic +1
children.description: A case for not always bringing your (primary) partner to events.

children.title: Does your partner need to be your best friend?
children.description: I do not think that your partner has to be also your best friend, but why?

children.title: Romance Feels Good, Which Is Sometimes Bad
children.description: Trying to understand why romance feels good and why that can lead to certain problems.

children.title: Polywise: A Deeper Dive into Navigating Open Relationships by Jessica Fern & David Cooley
children.description: From the author of the best-selling Polysecure: Attachment, Trauma and Consensual Nonmonogamy, a next-level guide for people looking to build secure attachment in nonmonogamous relationships.

children.title: Can you not have enough time for polyamory?
children.description: A rebuttal of an oft-cited reason that polyamory is a "bad fit" for someone.

children.title: Am I romantically attracted to my friend?
children.description: Trying to answer the age-old question that tortures many people in their relationships.

children.title: Relationship Limiting Beliefs
children.description: What are some beliefs that limit the possibilities in people's relationships?

children.title: Relationships Are About Sharing Life
children.description: Sharing is caring!

children.description: A look into dating beyond typical romantic relationships and how it complicates the search process.

children.description: A look into what RA as CNM might look like implemented, especially as an aromantic person.

children.description: A philosophical account of asexuality, sexual attraction, and sexual desire.

children.description: A personal exploration into Relationship Anarchy as a practice of Consensual Non-Monogamy.

children.title: We Need More IRL Friends to Lovers
children.description: People love to read the friends to lovers trope, but why not bring it into their own lives?

children.description: Romance and partnership go hand in hand conceptually, but do they need to be?

children.description: What would a world that goes beyond romance look like?

children.title: Romance as a Bad Organizing Principle
children.description: I believe that romance is not an optimal organizing principle for life or relationships.

children.title: The Allure of Convenience
children.description: I believe that part of the reason why the modern romantic relationship collapses the therapist, roommate, partner, and more into one is because it is an easier social structure. It is not the easiest to maintain however.

children.title: The Problems With Romantic Desire
children.description: I believe that romantic desire is a fickle master that is not the most optimal for organizing long-term relationships.

children.title: The Norms of Romance
children.description: Outlining the various norms of the stages of romance.

children.description: The Other Significant Others is an exploration of long-term platonic relationships in their various forms.

children.title: What Love Is: And What It Can Be by Carrie Ichikawa Jenkins
children.description: A rising star in philosophy examines the cultural, social, and scientific interpretations of love to answer one of our most enduring questions What is love?

children.title: Romantic abolitionism
children.description: Should we abolish the concept of romantic love?

children.title: Sexual inclination spectrum
children.description: Toward more exhaustive taxonomy for an attitude toward engaging in sexual activity.

children.title: Is compatibility discrete or continuous?
children.description: Yes/No vs % compatibility with a romantic partner.

children.title: Your partner shouldn't complete you
children.description: Soulmates and perfect halves don't exist and the idea may cause more harm than good.

children.title: Should breakups ever be unilateral?
children.description: Relationships are a union, but being dumped is usually a unilateral decision, which goes against the idea of a union.

children.description: How to have a constructive breakup conversation that leaves both parties better off.

children.title: Breakups as a last resort
children.description: Breakups should be treated only as the last option before exhausting other possible options.

children.title: Developing A Non-Hierarchical View of Friendships
children.description: Friendships are one of the most important things in our life, and I think that we could all benefit from re-evaluating how we think about them.

children.title: Love Languages Are Fake. So What?
children.description: Love Languages were created by a somewhat problematic marriage counselor and are not evidence-based/supported by science. Does this mean that Love Languages should be scrapped entirely? I think that with some rehabilitation, the concept of Love Languages can still provide a lot of utility to people.

children.description: A philosophical account of romance as personally defined niche of actions reserved for a certain person/people.

children.description: Applying Celine Nguyen’s mission to expand the market for literature to philosophy by instilling philosophical thinking and encouraging reading philosophy in the general public.

children.title: The Right to Sex: Feminism in the Twenty-First Century by Amia Srinivasan
children.description: Thrilling, sharp, and deeply humane, philosopher Amia Srinivasan's The Right to Sex: Feminism in the Twenty-First Century upends the way we discuss—or avoid discussing—the problems and politics of sex.

children.title: Accessible Philosophy Books
children.description: Philosophy doesn't have to be hard or boring!

children.title: Hermeneutic Labor
children.description: My notes, analysis, and extension of thought on the wonderful article about Hermeneutic Labor by Ellie Anderson.

children.title: Teaching Children Ethics
children.description: How can we teach children ethics in developmentally appropriate milestones?

children.title: How to Do Nothing by Jenny Odell
children.description: When the technologies we use every day collapse our experiences into 24/7 availability, platforms for personal branding, and products to be monetized, nothing can be quite so radical as… doing nothing. Here, Jenny Odell sends up a flare from the heart of Silicon Valley, delivering an action plan to resist capitalist narratives of productivity and techno-determinism, and to become more meaningfully connected in the process.

children.title: The Art of Gathering by Priya Parker
children.description: A transformative exploration of the power, purpose, and benefits of gatherings in our lives: at work, at school, at home and beyond.

children.title: The Meaning of Travel by Emily Thomas
children.description: How can we think more deeply about travel? This was the thought that inspired Emily Thomas to journey into the philosophy of travel, to explore the places where philosophy and travel intersect.

children.title: Being Mortal: Medicine and What Matters In The End by Atul Gawande
children.description: Being Mortal is a meditation on how people can better live with age-related frailty, serious illness, and approaching death.

children.title: Assholes: A Theory by Aaron James
children.description: In the New York Times bestseller Nick Hornby calls “helpful, stimulating, and very timely”, philosopher Aaron James presents a theory of the asshole that is both intellectually provocative and existentially necessary.

children.title: I Want to Die But I Want to Eat Tteokboki by Baek Seehee
children.description: Recording her dialogues with her psychiatrist over a twelve-week period, and expanding on each session with her own reflective micro-essays, Baek begins to disentangle the feedback loops, knee-jerk reactions, and harmful behaviors that keep her locked in a cycle of self-abuse. Part memoir, part self-help book, I Want to Die but I Want to Eat Tteokbokki is a book to keep close and to reach for in times of darkness. It will appeal to anyone who has ever felt alone or unjustified in their everyday despair.

children.title: Lying: Moral Choice in Public and Private Life by Sissela Bok
children.description: Is it ever all right to lie? A philosopher looks at lying and deception in public and private life—in government, medicine, law, academia, journalism, in the family and between friends.

children.title: The Moral Judgement of the Child by Jean Piaget
children.description: The Moral Judgement of the Child traces children's moral thinking from preschool to adolescence, tracing their concepts of lying, cheating, adult authority, punishment, and responsibility and offering important insights into how they learn -- or fail to learn -- the difference between right and wrong.

children.title: Uprooting the Flame
children.description: In this paper, I will argue that, following the advice of the Buddhists and Stoics, we should extirpate anger from ourselves for the betterment of ourselves, and to a greater extent, our society.

children.title: Incongruity Theory in the Philosophy of Humor
children.description: In this paper, I will first explain Platonism and its relation to mathematics and reconstruct arguments against it to show how mathematical objects ultimately cannot exist. I will then explore logicism and formalism in order to critically evaluate how they create truth for mathematical propositions and the problems that they have that could or should prevent mainstream philosophical adoption. Finally, I will explain Benacerraf’s structuralism and why I think it is the best Anti-Platonist explanation for the philosophical foundation of mathematics.

children.title: Evaluating Anti-Platonist Approaches to Mathematics
children.description: In this paper, I will first explain Platonism and its relation to mathematics and reconstruct arguments against it to show how mathematical objects ultimately cannot exist. I will then explore logicism and formalism in order to critically evaluate how they create truth for mathematical propositions and the problems that they have that could or should prevent mainstream philosophical adoption. Finally, I will explain Benacerraf’s structuralism and why I think it is the best Anti-Platonist explanation for the philosophical foundation of mathematics.

children.title: Holes (Pretty Good Movie/Pretty Tough Ontological Question)
children.description: In this essay I argue that holes cannot exist.

children.title: Theoretical Virtues of Major Theories of Time
children.description: In this paper, I have two main goals. The first of which is to develop an account of why people believe or choose to believe certain scientific and/or metaphysical theories, with an focus on theories of time. In particular, I will argue that people choose to believe intuition-preserving theories of time (intuition-preservation as a theoretical virtue). Additionally, I consider if this is a good thing or not, and whether people should evaluate theories on the basis of intuition-preservation. The second goal is to use the developed account to examine the theoretical virtues of some of the most popular competing metaphysical theories of time, namely: Presentism, Growing Block Theory, and Eternalism.

children.title: Ace: What Asexuality Reveals About Desire, Society, and the Meaning of Sex by Angela Chen
children.description: An engaging exploration of what it means to be asexual in a world that’s obsessed with sexual attraction, and what the ace perspective can teach all of us about desire and identity.

children.title: Philosophical Concepts Mentioned in R.F. Kuang's Katabasis
children.description: A list of philosophical concepts in R.F. Kuang's Katabasis, augmented by my recommended further readings.

children.title: Quarterlife: The Search for Self in Early Adulthood by Satya Doyle Byock
children.description: Quarterlife is a defining work that offers a compassionate roadmap toward finding understanding, happiness, and wholeness in adulthood.

children.title: Evolving as a Beer Drinker
children.description: My changing relationship with the kind of beers that I like over time.

children.title: Favorite Directors
children.description: A list of my favorite film directors.

children.description: The best Diet/Zero sodas

children.description: The best low calorie ice creams (very loose definition of low calorie).

children.description: An exploration of what it means to have a favorite brewery in the face of imperfection and changing tastes.

children.title: I Drank 1,000 Beers!

children.title: What Makes A Great Brewery Experience?

children.description: Reasons why you should give beer a chance.

children.title: The Walkability of Arlington, VA
children.description: My personal experience and opinions on Arlington, VA and its urban planning.

children.description: A list of my favorite breweries grouped by personal enjoyment and quality of beer.

children.description: A list of places to see and eat at in Philadelphia.

children.description: A guide on how to get into craft beer.

children.description: A list of cool spots in the DMV area if you enjoy craft beer.

children.description: A list of my favorite movies.

children.description: A list of my favorite TV shows (Western TV, anime, K-Dramas).

children.description: How and why I rate all the beers that I drink.

children.title: My Fast Food Orders
children.description: A list of my personal ideal fast food orders.

children.title: How To Buy Beer Like A Nerd
children.description: My methodology of buying craft beer in a way that diversifies risk and maximizes enjoyment.

children.title: Plant-Based Spinach Feta Crispy Gnocchi
children.description: Creamy with a touch of acid on crispy, crunchy gnocchi!

children.title: How much more expensive is eating plant-based?
children.description: What is the true cost of eating plants versus animals?

children.description: A list of snacks in order of best-to-worst CTP ratio.

children.description: A list of alt-proteins in order of best-to-worst CTP ratio.

children.title: Eating Animals by Jonathan Safran Foer
children.description: Part memoir and part investigative report, Eating Animals is the groundbreaking moral examination of vegetarianism, farming, and the food we eat every day that inspired the documentary of the same name.

children.title: The Effects of Different Reducetarian Splits
children.description: How should you structure your (non-)meat eating as a Reducetarian?

children.title: Getting People to Eat Less Meat
children.description: How can we get people to eat less meat?

children.title: Reducetarian
children.description: Placing my Vegan at Home system in the greater context of the Reducetarian movement.

children.title: Lazy Vegan Meals
children.description: A list of vegan snacks and low-prep meals for when I don't feel like cooking.

children.title: Vegan Substitutions
children.description: Item or ingredient food substitutions for vegan alternatives to popular foods.

children.title: Veganize It!
children.description: Some ways to make something vegan via substitution.

children.title: Ginger and Peach Tea Mocktail
children.description: A fruity, carbonated mocktail with nice ginger flavor.

children.title: Coke Latte
children.description: My take on a Coke with coffee in a cocktail with cream liqueur.

children.title: Smokey Spicy Pineapple Margarita
children.description: A fruity, smokey, and spicy Margarita that is sure to delight.

children.title: Honey Ginger Chili Garlic Glazed Carrots
children.description: A carrot full of lively flavors!

children.title: Crispy Pan-Fried Tteokboki
children.description: A spicy, sweet, and savory version of tteokboki that is pan fried and crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside.

children.title: Spam Chorizo Quesadilla
children.description: Converting Spam into chorizo for a nice cheesy, salty, fatty quesadilla.

children.title: Salad Greens Namul
children.description: A quick and easy take on Korean namul with leftover salad greens.

children.title: Ground Beef Curry Noodles
children.description: A non-traditional take on Japanese curry with noodles that is still hearty & warming.

children.title: Tzatziki Mustard Charred Cabbage Salad
children.description: A salad with charred cabbage + romaine lettuce for balance with a tangy tzatziki mustard dressing.

children.title: Vegan At Home
children.description: My system for eating vegan at home, but non-vegan outside of the house.

children.title: Gym Rats Are The Perfect Reducetarian Target
children.description: A case to focus on people who are heavily involved in personal fitness for Reducetarianism.

children.title: Perfect Days (2023): Review
children.description: Hirayama cleans public toilets in Tokyo, lives his life in simplicity and daily tranquility. Some encounters also lead him to reflect on himself.

children.title: Happyend (2024): Review
children.description: Friends Yuta and Kou, about to graduate, sneak into school. They pull off a terrible prank and face unforeseen consequences as graduation approaches.

children.title: Cinema as Poetry and My Taste in Film
children.description: A lens of film interpretation that I employ and details about things I look for in films I like.

children.title: The Moral Philosophy of The Drama (2026)
children.description: In this essay, I aim to pull apart the entanglement of moral issues presented in this narrative, especially in regards to the moral intutions at play.

children.title: About Endlessness (2019): Review
children.description: A reflection on human life in all its beauty and cruelty, its splendor and banality, guided by a Scheherazade-esque narrator. Inconsequential moments have the same significance as historical events. Simultaneously an ode and a lament, presents a kaleidoscope of all that is eternally human, an infinite story of the vulnerability of existence.

children.title: Ex Machina (2015): Review
children.description: A young programmer is selected to participate in a ground-breaking experiment in synthetic intelligence by evaluating the human qualities of a highly advanced humanoid A.I.

children.title: Finally Using My DVDs
children.description: A reflection on starting to watch my collected DVDs.

children.title: Scenes From A Marriage (1974): Review
children.description: Johan and Marianne's marital life appears perfect on the surface. However, their marriage heads towards divorce when Johan becomes involved with another woman.

children.title: The End of the Tour (2015): Review
children.description: The story of the five-day interview between Rolling Stone reporter David Lipsky and acclaimed novelist David Foster Wallace, which took place right after the 1996 publication of Wallace’s groundbreaking epic novel, 'Infinite Jest.'

children.title: Evil Does Not Exist (2023): Review
children.description: Takumi and his daughter live in Mizubiki Village, close to Tokyo. One day, the village inhabitants become aware of a plan to build a glamping site near Takumi's house, offering city residents a comfortable escape to nature.

children.title: Caught By The Tides (2025): Review
children.description: Years after her boyfriend left her for the big city and promised to bring her there after he’s settled down, a Chinese woman sets out on a journey to be reunited with him.

children.title: Marjorie Prime (2017): Review
children.description: A service which creates holographic projections of late family members allows an elderly woman to spend time with a younger version of her deceased husband.

children.title: Yi Yi (2000): Review
children.description: Each member of a Taipei family asks hard questions about life’s meaning as they experience everyday quandaries. NJ is morose: his brother owes him money, his mother-in-law is in a coma, his wife suffers a spiritual crisis when she finds her life a blank and his business partners make bad decisions.

children.title: The Seventh Seal (1957): Review
children.description: A medieval knight plays a game of chess with the personification of Death who has come to take his life.

children.title: Warfare (2025): Review
children.description: A platoon of American Navy SEALs on a surveillance mission gone wrong in insurgent territory. A boots-on-the-ground story of modern warfare and brotherhood, told in real time and based on the memory of the people who lived it.

children.title: A Pigeon Sat On A Branch Reflecting On Existence (2014): Review
children.description: An absurdist, surrealistic and shocking pitch-black comedy, which moves freely from nightmare to fantasy to hilariously deadpan humour as it muses on man’s perpetual inhumanity to man.

children.title: Materialists (2025): Review
children.description: A young, ambitious New York City matchmaker (Dakota Johnson) finds herself torn between the perfect match (Pedro Pascal) and her imperfect ex (Chris Evans).

children.title: By The Stream (2024): Review
children.description: After a scandal breaks out among several students involved in the production of a skit, a lecturer at a South Korean women’s college brings in her uncle, a famous actor, to bring the work to fruition.
